enrichment.short_description: Bagisto is a free, open-source Laravel e-commerce platform for building online stores, multi-vendor marketplaces, and B2B shops.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bagisto is an open-source Laravel e-commerce platform for marketplaces and B2B stores, with optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace
features.description: Built-in tools for running a marketplace with multiple sellers on a single storefront.

features.title: B2B and B2C commerce
features.description: Supports both direct-to-consumer storefronts and B2B selling scenarios from the same codebase.

features.title: Headless, API-first architecture
features.description: Exposes an API layer so the storefront can be decoupled from the commerce backend.

features.title: Point of Sale (POS)
features.description: Includes a POS module for selling through physical/in-person channels alongside the online store.

features.title: Omnichannel selling
features.description: Lets merchants sell across website, mobile, and POS channels from one admin.

features.title: Multi-tenant SaaS support
features.description: Can be configured to run as a multi-tenant SaaS deployment for hosting multiple stores.

pros: Free and open source with full source code access
Large GitHub community (26K+ stars)
Built on the widely used Laravel PHP framework, easing customization for Laravel developers
Supports multi-vendor, B2B, and headless use cases out of the box
Optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting removes the need to self-host
cons: Self-hosting requires PHP/Laravel server administration skills
Bagisto Cloud Hosting pricing is not published on the public site
Advanced enterprise features typically require paid services from Webkul

faqs.question: Is Bagisto free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bagisto's core platform is open source and free to download and self-host.

faqs.question: What framework is Bagisto built on?
faqs.answer: Bagisto is built on the Laravel PHP framework.

faqs.question: Can Bagisto run a multi-vendor marketplace?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bagisto includes multi-vendor marketplace functionality.

faqs.question: Does Bagisto offer hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Webkul offers Bagisto Cloud Hosting as a managed alternative to self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Multi-vendor marketplaces
use_cases.description: Merchants can launch a marketplace where multiple sellers list and manage their own products.

use_cases.title: B2B storefronts
use_cases.description: Businesses can set up B2B-specific pricing, catalogs, and workflows.

use_cases.title: Headless commerce projects
use_cases.description: Development teams can use Bagisto's API layer to power custom frontends.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bagisto?
paragraphs: Bagisto is an open-source e-commerce platform built on the Laravel PHP framework. It is designed for building online stores, multi-vendor marketplaces, and B2B shops, and can also be deployed in a headless, API-first configuration.
The project is maintained by Webkul and is distributed as free, self-hostable software, with an optional managed Bagisto Cloud Hosting service for merchants who don't want to run their own servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bagisto is for
paragraphs: Bagisto suits developers and agencies comfortable with Laravel who want a customizable, self-hosted e-commerce foundation, as well as merchants launching multi-vendor marketplaces or B2B storefronts.

enrichment.short_description: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP shopping cart platform with a marketplace of over 13,000 extensions and themes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP e-commerce platform with a large marketplace of extensions, themes, and payment gateways.

features.title: Extension marketplace
features.description: Official marketplace offering over 13,000 modules and themes to extend store functionality.

features.title: Multi-language and multi-currency
features.description: Built-in support for running stores in multiple languages and currencies.

features.title: Multiple payment gateways
features.description: Integrates with gateways including PayPal, Authorize.Net, Amazon Payments, and Klarna.

features.title: Order and catalog management
features.description: Core admin tools for managing products, orders, and customers.

features.title: GPL open-source license
features.description: Distributed under the GNU General Public License, free to download, modify, and self-host.

pros: Completely free, open-source core software with no licensing fee
One of the largest e-commerce extension marketplaces (13,000+ listings)
No monthly platform fees for the base software
Long-running project with an active developer community
GPL license permits full source code modification
cons: Requires self-hosting and ongoing PHP/server maintenance
Many advanced features depend on paid third-party extensions and themes
No official managed hosting or cloud plan from OpenCart itself

faqs.question: Is OpenCart free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenCart's core software is free to download and use under the GNU General Public License.

faqs.question: Does OpenCart charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: OpenCart itself does not charge monthly platform or transaction fees; costs come from hosting, extensions, and themes.

faqs.question: How many extensions does OpenCart have?
faqs.answer: OpenCart's official marketplace lists over 13,000 extensions and themes.

faqs.question: What language is OpenCart written in?
faqs.answer: OpenCart is written in PHP and supports MySQL, MariaDB, and PostgreSQL databases.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted online stores
use_cases.description: Merchants who want full control over hosting and infrastructure can run OpenCart on their own servers.

use_cases.title: Agency-built storefronts
use_cases.description: Agencies can use OpenCart's extension marketplace to assemble custom stores for clients.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenCart?
paragraphs: OpenCart is a free, open-source PHP shopping cart platform originally revived and rewritten by Daniel Kerr, with its first stable release in 2009. It is self-hosted and distributed under the GNU General Public License.

The platform is supported by a large marketplace of over 13,000 extensions and themes, allowing merchants to add payment gateways, shipping methods, and storefront customizations without core code changes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenCart is for
paragraphs: OpenCart fits merchants and agencies who want a free, self-hosted PHP e-commerce foundation and are willing to manage their own server and select extensions to fill in additional functionality.

enrichment.short_description: Thirty Bees is a free, open-source e-commerce platform forked from PrestaShop 1.6, offering 500+ built-in features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Thirty Bees is an open-source e-commerce platform forked from PrestaShop 1.6, with over 500 built-in features and an add-on marketplace.

features.title: Flexible checkout options
features.description: Supports both one-page and five-step checkout flows.

features.title: Responsive Bootstrap design
features.description: Storefronts are built on Bootstrap for responsive display across devices.

features.title: Multi-site and multi-currency
features.description: Supports running multiple sites and currencies from one installation.

features.title: Built-in SEO tools
features.description: Includes SEO-oriented configuration options for storefronts.

features.title: PDF invoice generation
features.description: Generates PDF invoices for orders natively.

features.title: PrestaShop 1.6 module compatibility
features.description: Retains compatibility with many modules and themes built for PrestaShop 1.6.

pros: Free and open source
Forked from the stable PrestaShop 1.6 codebase, retaining much of its module/theme ecosystem
Ships with 500+ built-in features
Actively maintained, with a version 1.6 release in November 2024
Emphasizes speed and security in its architecture
cons: Smaller community and ecosystem than PrestaShop or larger platforms
Some modules, themes, and support are paid add-ons via its marketplace
Self-hosting requires ongoing server and PHP maintenance

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees related to PrestaShop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Thirty Bees is an open-source fork of PrestaShop 1.6, created in 2017 to maintain a stable, compatible alternative.

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core platform is free and open source; paid modules, themes, and support are available through its marketplace.

faqs.question: Is Thirty Bees still maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, the project released version 1.6 in November 2024, indicating ongoing development.

faqs.question: Can I use PrestaShop 1.6 modules with Thirty Bees?
faqs.answer: Thirty Bees is designed to remain compatible with many PrestaShop 1.6 modules and themes.

use_cases.title: Migrating off PrestaShop 1.6
use_cases.description: Merchants on PrestaShop 1.6 who want continued stability can migrate using Thirty Bees' built-in migration tooling.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted online stores
use_cases.description: Merchants who want a free, self-hosted store with a large built-in feature set.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Thirty Bees?
paragraphs: Thirty Bees is an open-source e-commerce platform that began in 2017 as a fork of PrestaShop 1.6, created by developers who wanted to keep building on that stable codebase after PrestaShop moved to a less backward-compatible 1.7 release.

The platform ships with over 500 built-in features and remains compatible with many PrestaShop 1.6 modules and themes, while continuing its own independent development, including a version 1.6 release in November 2024.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Thirty Bees is for
paragraphs: Thirty Bees is aimed at merchants and developers who used PrestaShop 1.6 and want a compatible, actively maintained, free open-source alternative.

enrichment.short_description: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless Ruby on Rails e-commerce platform with a Next.js storefront and an optional Enterprise support tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless e-commerce platform built on Ruby on Rails with a Next.js storefront and enterprise support option.

features.title: REST API and TypeScript SDK
features.description: Exposes REST APIs and a TypeScript SDK for building custom storefronts.

features.title: Next.js storefront
features.description: Ships with a production-ready Next.js storefront that connects to the Rails backend.

features.title: B2B wholesale portals
features.description: Supports custom price lists and approval workflows for B2B buyers.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace support
features.description: Can be configured to run multi-vendor marketplace scenarios.

features.title: Multi-tenant architecture
features.description: Supports multi-tenant deployments for embedding commerce into SaaS products.

features.title: Payment gateway abstraction
features.description: Integrates with gateways including Stripe, Adyen, and PayPal.

features.title: Configurable promotions engine
features.description: Includes a pricing and promotions engine for discounts and campaigns.

pros: BSD 3-Clause open-source license with full source code access
No GMV cuts or per-transaction platform fees on the Community Edition
Large community: 15K+ GitHub stars and 2M+ downloads
Used by notable brands including Bookshop, Bonobos, and GoDaddy
Headless architecture with a ready-made Next.js storefront
cons: Requires Ruby on Rails development expertise to self-host and customize
Enterprise Edition pricing is not published publicly
Setting up the Next.js storefront adds implementation complexity beyond the API backend

faqs.question: Is Spree Commerce free?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source under a BSD 3-Clause license; an Enterprise Edition with dedicated support is available separately.

faqs.question: What tech stack does Spree use?
faqs.answer: Spree's backend is built on Ruby on Rails, with a Next.js storefront and TypeScript SDKs for integration.

faqs.question: Does Spree charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: Spree states the Community Edition has no GMV cuts or transaction fees.

faqs.question: Who uses Spree Commerce?
faqs.answer: Spree lists customers including Bookshop, Bonobos, GoDaddy, and KFC among its adopters.

use_cases.title: Headless B2C storefronts
use_cases.description: Teams can build custom storefronts against Spree's REST API and TypeScript SDK, or use its Next.js starter.

use_cases.title: B2B wholesale portals
use_cases.description: Businesses can run B2B ordering with custom price lists and approval workflows.

use_cases.title: Embedded multi-tenant commerce
use_cases.description: SaaS products can embed Spree's multi-tenant commerce engine.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spree Commerce?
paragraphs: Spree Commerce is an open-source, headless e-commerce platform with a Ruby on Rails backend, a REST API, a TypeScript SDK, and a production-ready Next.js storefront. The Community Edition is BSD 3-Clause licensed and free to self-host.
For businesses that need dedicated support, Spree also offers an Enterprise Edition. The project reports 15K+ GitHub stars, 2M+ downloads, and use by brands such as Bookshop, Bonobos, and GoDaddy.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spree Commerce is for
paragraphs: Spree suits developers and enterprises building headless, API-driven commerce, including B2B wholesale, multi-vendor marketplace, and multi-tenant SaaS scenarios, who are comfortable with a Ruby on Rails backend.

name: Reaction Commerce
skip_reason: Project has been discontinued/rebranded. The official website reactioncommerce.com is unreachable (DNS resolution failure), and the project's GitHub repository (github.com/reactioncommerce/reaction) explicitly states 'Project has been discontinued' and that the codebase continues only as Mailchimp Open Commerce under a different name/owner.

enrichment.short_description: Solidus is a free, open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce framework forked from Spree in 2015, maintained by Nebulab and the community.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Solidus is an open-source Ruby on Rails e-commerce framework forked from Spree, with an optional fully hosted Solidus Cloud Enterprise plan.

features.title: Merchandising and personalization
features.description: Supports personalization using first-party data, upsells, and content integration.

features.title: Native subscriptions engine
features.description: Includes a subscription engine for curated boxes and recurring purchase models.

features.title: Custom promotions logic
features.description: Allows custom promotion rules beyond simple discount codes.

features.title: Internationalization
features.description: Supports multi-language and localization for global storefronts.

features.title: REST and GraphQL APIs
features.description: Provides both REST and GraphQL APIs for building custom frontends and integrations.

features.title: Starter theme and admin dashboard
features.description: Ships with a starter storefront theme and an admin dashboard for store management.

pros: Free and open source with full source code access
Highly customizable Ruby on Rails framework aimed at complex/custom commerce needs
Native subscriptions support built in
Both REST and GraphQL APIs available
Maintained by Nebulab and an active open-source community
cons: Requires Ruby on Rails development expertise to implement and customize
Solidus Cloud (Enterprise) pricing is not publicly listed
Smaller ecosystem than larger hosted platforms

faqs.question: Is Solidus free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus is a free, open-source framework that can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: What is Solidus built on?
faqs.answer: Solidus is built on Ruby on Rails and was forked from Spree in 2015.

faqs.question: Does Solidus offer managed hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus Cloud is offered as a fully hosted Enterprise option with dedicated support.

faqs.question: Who maintains Solidus?
faqs.answer: Solidus is maintained by Nebulab and the open-source community.

use_cases.title: Custom-built brand storefronts
use_cases.description: DTC and CPG brands needing deep customization beyond standard platforms can build on Solidus.

use_cases.title: Subscription commerce
use_cases.description: Merchants running curated-box or recurring subscription models can use Solidus' native subscriptions engine.

use_cases.title: Fully hosted enterprise commerce
use_cases.description: Larger organizations can use Solidus Cloud for a fully hosted deployment with dedicated support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Solidus?
paragraphs: Solidus is an open-source e-commerce framework built on Ruby on Rails. It was created in 2015 as a fork of Spree by the team behind the Bonobos store, after Spree's development direction changed, and it is now maintained by Nebulab and the open-source community.

Solidus targets brands that need deep customization, offering merchandising/personalization tools, a native subscriptions engine, custom promotions logic, and both REST and GraphQL APIs. A fully hosted Solidus Cloud Enterprise option is also available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Solidus is for
paragraphs: Solidus is aimed at DTC, CPG, fashion, subscription, and marketplace brands that need custom commerce logic beyond what standard SaaS platforms offer, and have (or can hire) Ruby on Rails development capacity.

enrichment.short_description: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module for the Drupal CMS, maintained by Centarro since 2010.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module that integrates content, product, and order management within the Drupal CMS.

features.title: Multi-site, multi-language, multi-currency
features.description: Supports running multiple sites, languages, and currencies from a Drupal installation.

features.title: Advanced product display pages
features.description: Leverages Drupal's media library and layout builder for product pages.

features.title: Search API integration
features.description: Integrates with Drupal's Search API for keyword search and faceted filtering.

features.title: Promotion and coupon management
features.description: Includes tools for configuring promotions and coupon codes.

features.title: Custom fields and layouts
features.description: Merchants can configure custom fields and page layouts using Drupal's field and layout systems.

features.title: Third-party API integrations
features.description: Integrates with external applications via APIs and Drupal's event system.

pros: Free and open source
Deep integration with Drupal's content management and layout tools
Actively maintained, with Commerce Core 3.2.0 released in September 2025
Flexible custom field and layout configuration for merchandising
Paid consulting and support available through maintainer Centarro
cons: Requires Drupal CMS knowledge to implement and maintain
Smaller dedicated e-commerce community compared to standalone commerce platforms
Self-hosting and Drupal maintenance are the merchant's responsibility

faqs.question: Is Drupal Commerce free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Drupal Commerce is free, open-source software; paid consulting and development services are available separately through Centarro.

faqs.question: What platform does Drupal Commerce run on?
faqs.answer: Drupal Commerce is a module that runs on top of the Drupal content management system.

faqs.question: Who maintains Drupal Commerce?
faqs.answer: Drupal Commerce is maintained by Centarro, which has been involved with the project since its creation in 2010.

faqs.question: What was the latest Drupal Commerce release?
faqs.answer: Commerce Core 3.2.0 was released on September 10, 2025.

use_cases.title: Content-driven commerce sites
use_cases.description: Organizations that already run Drupal for content can add commerce without switching platforms.

use_cases.title: Multi-site, multi-language stores
use_cases.description: Businesses needing multiple storefronts in different languages/currencies from one codebase.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Drupal Commerce?
paragraphs: Drupal Commerce is a free, open-source e-commerce module for the Drupal content management system, maintained by Centarro since the project's creation in 2010. It integrates product, content, and order management within a single Drupal site.

The platform supports multi-site, multi-language, and multi-currency stores, and takes advantage of Drupal's media library, layout builder, and Search API for product display and search. Centarro also offers paid consulting and development services.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Drupal Commerce is for
paragraphs: Drupal Commerce fits agencies and organizations already building on Drupal who want to add e-commerce capability using the same CMS, content, and layout tooling they already use.

enrichment.short_description: GrandNode is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform released under the GPL-3 license, with optional paid support packages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GrandNode is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform under GPL-3, supporting B2B, B2C, marketplace, and headless commerce.

features.title: B2C and B2B store management
features.description: Supports customer grouping and custom pricing for both B2C and B2B selling.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace
features.description: Includes marketplace functionality for running multi-vendor storefronts.

features.title: Cross-channel integration
features.description: Integrates with channels such as Amazon and eBay, including inventory sync.

features.title: Booking and reservation system
features.description: Supports booking/reservation-based products such as accommodations and services.

features.title: E-learning and digital products
features.description: Supports selling digital products and paid courses.

features.title: Headless commerce API
features.description: Provides an API architecture for headless commerce implementations.

pros: Free and open source under the GPL-3 license
Built on the modern ASP.NET Core / .NET stack
Covers multiple business models (B2B, marketplace, bookings, digital products) in one platform
Headless API architecture for custom frontends
Optional premium support packages available for teams that want vendor support
cons: Requires .NET development expertise to self-host and customize
Smaller community than PHP-based open-source e-commerce platforms
Specific premium support pricing is not published on the public site

faqs.question: Is GrandNode free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode's core platform is free and open source under the GPL-3 license.

faqs.question: What technology is GrandNode built on?
faqs.answer: GrandNode is built on ASP.NET Core (.NET).

faqs.question: Does GrandNode support B2B selling?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode supports B2B store management including customer grouping and custom pricing.

faqs.question: Is paid support available for GrandNode?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrandNode offers premium support packages and custom services in addition to its free open-source core.

use_cases.title: Multi-model commerce on .NET
use_cases.description: Teams standardized on .NET can run B2B, B2C, marketplace, or booking-based commerce from one platform.

use_cases.title: Headless commerce backends
use_cases.description: Developers can use GrandNode's API to power custom storefronts.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GrandNode?
paragraphs: GrandNode is a free, open-source e-commerce platform built on ASP.NET Core and released under the GPL-3 license. It is designed to support multiple business models, including B2B, B2C, multi-vendor marketplace, bookings/reservations, and digital products/e-learning.
The core platform is self-hosted and free to use, with optional premium support packages and custom implementation services available for teams that need vendor support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GrandNode is for
paragraphs: GrandNode fits developers and agencies working in the .NET ecosystem who want a single open-source platform capable of handling B2B, marketplace, booking, or digital-product commerce.

enrichment.short_description: nopCommerce is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform first released in 2008, downloaded more than 3 million times.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: nopCommerce is a free, open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform released under the nopCommerce Public License, with optional paid support.

features.title: ASP.NET Core architecture
features.description: Built on ASP.NET Core with an MS SQL Server backend.

features.title: Multi-store support
features.description: Supports running multiple stores from a single installation.

features.title: Plugin and theme marketplace
features.description: Offers a marketplace of plugins and themes to extend store functionality.

features.title: Built-in SEO tools
features.description: Includes SEO-friendly URL and metadata configuration.

features.title: Discount and promotion engine
features.description: Includes tools for configuring discounts and promotional campaigns.

features.title: Multi-currency and multi-language
features.description: Supports selling in multiple currencies and languages.

pros: Free to download and use commercially, with no license fee
Long-running project first released in 2008, with 3M+ downloads
Actively developed, with version 4.90.6 released in July 2026
Extensive plugin and theme marketplace
Optional premium support available for teams that need it
cons: Requires ASP.NET Core / .NET hosting expertise to self-host
Many themes and plugins in the marketplace are paid add-ons
Distributed under a custom nopCommerce Public License rather than a standard OSI license

faqs.question: Is nopCommerce free?
faqs.answer: Yes, nopCommerce is free to download, install, and use commercially, released under the nopCommerce Public License.

faqs.question: What technology does nopCommerce use?
faqs.answer: nopCommerce is built on ASP.NET Core with an MS SQL Server backend.

faqs.question: How long has nopCommerce been around?
faqs.answer: Development started in 2008, and the platform has been downloaded more than 3 million times.

faqs.question: Does nopCommerce offer paid support?
faqs.answer: Yes, optional premium support and paid plugins/themes are available alongside the free core platform.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted .NET storefronts
use_cases.description: Merchants and agencies standardized on Microsoft/.NET infrastructure can run nopCommerce as their store platform.

use_cases.title: Multi-store retail operations
use_cases.description: Businesses running several storefronts can manage them from a single nopCommerce installation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is nopCommerce?
paragraphs: nopCommerce is a free, open-source e-commerce platform built on ASP.NET Core with an MS SQL Server backend. Development began in 2008, and the platform has since been downloaded more than 3 million times.

The software is distributed under the nopCommerce Public License and is free to download and use commercially. An ecosystem of plugins, themes, and optional premium support is available for merchants who want additional functionality or vendor assistance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who nopCommerce is for
paragraphs: nopCommerce suits merchants and development teams working within a Microsoft/.NET technology stack who want a free, self-hosted, actively maintained e-commerce platform.

enrichment.short_description: Snipcart is a drop-in shopping cart and checkout platform for any website, priced at 2% per transaction with a $20/month minimum.
enrichment.starting_price: 2% per transaction (minimum $20/month for stores under $1,000 USD in monthly sales)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snipcart is a drop-in shopping cart and checkout platform for any website, charging 2% per transaction with a free testing environment.

features.title: Drop-in cart snippet
features.description: Add a shopping cart and checkout flow to an existing site with an HTML/JS snippet, no redirect required.

features.title: Physical and digital products
features.description: Supports selling both physical goods and digital products.

features.title: Abandoned cart recovery
features.description: Includes tools to recover abandoned shopping carts.

features.title: Multi-currency and tax management
features.description: Handles multiple currencies and tax calculation for orders.

features.title: Multiple payment gateways
features.description: Integrates with several payment gateways for checkout.

features.title: API and webhooks
features.description: Provides an API and webhooks for custom integrations.

pros: Works with virtually any tech stack, including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel sites
Free, unlimited testing environment with no credit card required
Pure usage-based pricing with no separate platform subscription fee
Unlimited products, variants, and domains on the single plan
Built-in abandoned cart recovery and tax handling
cons: 2% transaction fee applies on top of payment gateway fees
$20/month minimum fee for stores under $1,000 in monthly sales
Requires developer integration into an existing site rather than providing a full storefront builder

faqs.question: How much does Snipcart cost?
faqs.answer: Snipcart charges 2% per transaction, with a $20/month minimum fee for stores generating under $1,000 USD in monthly sales.

faqs.question: Is Snipcart free to test?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipcart's testing/development environment is free with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What platforms does Snipcart work with?
faqs.answer: Snipcart is designed to work with any tech stack, including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel.

faqs.question: Can Snipcart sell digital products?
faqs.answer: Yes, Snipcart supports selling both physical and digital products.

use_cases.title: Adding a cart to a custom-built site
use_cases.description: Developers can add checkout functionality to a static or custom site without building a cart from scratch.

use_cases.title: Selling on a Jamstack or headless site
use_cases.description: Sites built with static site generators or headless CMSs can add commerce via Snipcart's drop-in cart.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snipcart?
paragraphs: Snipcart is a shopping cart and checkout platform that developers add to an existing website via an HTML/JS snippet, rather than requiring a full storefront rebuild. It supports physical and digital products, abandoned cart recovery, multi-currency and tax handling, and multiple payment gateways.

Pricing is usage-based: Snipcart charges 2% per transaction, with a $20/month minimum for stores under $1,000 in monthly sales, and offers a free, unlimited testing environment with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snipcart is for
paragraphs: Snipcart is built for developers and agencies who want to bolt e-commerce functionality onto an existing site — including Jamstack, React, Vue, WordPress, and Laravel builds — without adopting a full standalone e-commerce platform.
